text:
Admission Date:  2019-11-18     Discharge Date:  1973-11-12

Date of Birth:   1993-12-21     Sex:  F

Service:

This is an addendum on the discharge summary initially
dictated on 10-19 for anticipated discharge at that time.

Patient's discharge was deferred on 10-19 secondary to new
onset of wound drainage.  Wound cultures were obtained and
Gram stains were negative.  Over the next 24 hours the
drainage increased.  Patient remained afebrile.  She was
found the following day by her nurse Lori Guzman neck
bleeding.  Pressure was applied and the patient was
transferred to the VICU.

She underwent a right neck exploration.  The patch was
intact.  There was diffuse oozing from the wound and skin.
There was no vessel puncture.  No specific site of bleeding.
The patient was returned to 4254 William Pike Suite 026
North Zacharybury, KY 39948-7926 to be extubated, and had the
second episode of bleeding in the PACU and returned to the
operating room.  Findings were the same.  Neck was closed.

Patient's post-procedure hematocrit was 24.  She required
transfusion with 2 units of packed cells.  Patient remained
in the PACU overnight.  Post-transfusion hematocrit was 28.0.
Josylin followed the patient during her hospital stay to
manage her diabetic management.  The resident was called to
see the patient because of appearing increase of swelling in
the neck region with thrill.  The trachea was midline.  There
was no respiratory decompensation.  Repeat hematocrit was
obtained and this was 30.0.  A nitroglycerin support for
blood pressure control maintained her systolic blood pressure
less than 150.

Patient was continued to require IV nitroglycerin for blood
pressure control over the next 24-48 hours.  Her
antihypertensive drug regime was readjusted multiple times to
maintain a systolic less than 150.

An ultrasound of the neck was obtained on 04-13 which shows a
large pseudoaneurysm measuring 6.9 x 4.6 cm sagittally in the
right neck.  Patient was continued on Vancomycin and she
underwent on 01-24 repeat carotid surgery with excision of
pseudoaneurysm and an interpositional greater saphenous vein
grafting to the right common internal carotid.  She tolerated
the procedure well.  She was transferred to the Post
Anesthesia Care Unit in stable condition.

She remained hemodynamically stable.  Blood pressure under
reasonable control systolic 158.  Hematocrit was 28.3.
Electrolytes were stable.  Her coags were normal.  She did
require insulin drips intraoperatively, but this was
discontinued.  She was transferred to the SICU for a
monitoring overnight on 01-24.  She was extubated and weaned
from her nitroglycerin and was transferred to the VICU on
2008-07-01.

On 1996-12-06 infectious disease recommended continuing six
week course of Vancomycin which started on 01-24.  Blood
pressure stabilized to 135/50.  She continued to do well.
Her neck dressing remained clean, dry, and intact.  The wound
was intact.  There was no swelling or hematoma.
Neurologically she continued to do well.  Renal function
returned to baseline with a creatinine of 0.8 and a BUN of
17.  Recommendations were that she had angioedema symptoms
with Zestril and avoid ACE inhibitors, patient can be on ARBs
if indicated.

At the time of discharge, the patient was in stable
condition, neurologically intact.  Wound was clean, dry, and
intact.  PICC line was functional.  Patient was discharged to
home and she would follow up with Dr. Randall in two weeks'
time or sooner if indicated.  She would continue her IV
antibiotics for a total of six weeks and this will be done at
Marilyn Parker on an outpatient basis.  She should have weekly
complete blood count, BUN, creatinine, and peak and troph
levels done.  The patient should follow up in two weeks' time
with Dr. Randall.

DISCHARGE MEDICATIONS:
1. NPH insulin 35 units q am with sliding scales at
breakfast, lunch, and dinner with regular insulin.  Glucoses
less than 151 no insulin, 151-200 3, 201-255, 251-300 7,
301-359, greater than 351 11 units at breakfast.  Dinner
scale was the same as breakfast scale.  Lunch scale was
glucoses less than 200 no insulin, 201-250 2 units, 251-300 5
units, 301-350 7 units, greater than 351 9 units.  Bedtime
insulin is glucoses greater than 300-350 2 units, greater
than 351 3 units.
2. Diflucan 150 mg q day times a total of 17 days, this was
done on 05-04.  This should be discontinued on 02-19.
3. Aspirin 325 mg daily.
4. Vancomycin 1 gram q 24 hours; this was started on 01-24
and this will continue for a total of six weeks.
5. Protonix 40 mg q day.
6. Colace 100 mg North Bay Vacavalley Hospital.
7. Cardizem 30 mg qid.
8. Lopressor 100 mg tid.
9. Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg q day.
10. Diovan 160 mg q day.

DISCHARGE DIAGNOSES:
1. Right neck hematoma status post exploration and evacuation
of clot.
2. Pseudoaneurysm status post resection with interpositional
graft greater saphenous vein to the right carotid.
3. Hypertension controlled ATN secondary to Staphylococcus
infection and sensitivity to Zestril corrected.
4. Blood loss anemia corrected.
5. Urinary yeast infection treated.






                          [**First Name11 (Name Pattern1) **] Brown, M.D.  89739

Dictated By:Morales
